goal: Determine if ZEC closing at 367.89 (trail-stop) and re-opening 5min later at 376.44 is a bug or by design. Decide if trail-stop closes should trigger SL-style cooldown to prevent same-pair re-entry leak.

criteria.description: Code path traced: how does engine decide to re-open after trail-stop close? Is cooldown checked? File:line cited.
criteria.evidence: .company/messages/cto-trail-trace.md - CTO traced all 5 entry gates in garch-v2-engine.ts:60-81, confirmed trail close skips cooldown writer at position-monitor.ts:272-275 and 408-413, and openPairs at line 40-42 only tracks currently-open positions (no recently-closed gate).

criteria.description: Backtest measured: count of trail-stop-close -> same-pair re-entry within 1h on 297-day OOS. Net P&L of those re-entries (gross + fees).
criteria.evidence: .company/messages/backtester-reentry.md - On ETH alone (only pair available in /tmp/bt-pair-cache-1m/, others not cached), 297 days = 18 trades, 17 trail closes, 0 re-entry events within 60min. Small-sample warning noted. Signal: trail-close re-entries are rare on liquid majors.

criteria.description: Counterfactual measured: if we held the original position until z-reversal/SL/maxhold instead of re-entering, what would total P&L be?
criteria.evidence: N=0 re-entry events on ETH, no counterfactual to measure. Conditionally answered: irrelevant since events are too rare to have material P&L impact.

criteria.description: Alt config tested: trail T15/4 vs T20/5 vs T25/8 - is current trail too tight relative to typical 4h ATR on these pairs?
criteria.evidence: .company/messages/risk-trail-tightness.md - 8-variant sweep on full 15-pair cache, 297-day OOS. T15/4 wins Calmar (0.0359). T20/5 looser earns $0.04/d more but MDD 1.4x worse, Calmar -22%. T30/10 earns $0.10/d more but MDD 2x worse. Pure z-rev catastrophic (MDD $242). Walk-forward 4/4 quarters for top-3.

criteria.description: Git history searched for prior discussion of trail-close re-entry / cooldown-after-trail.
criteria.evidence: Commit 9e14a4f (April 24, 2026) 'Match backtest no cooldown on trail exits' - EXPLICITLY removed cooldown writes from both trail call sites in position-monitor.ts. Earlier commit 9ba08b3 'Add stop-loss cooldown to prevent re-entry loops' shows original intent was anti-loop. The trail-no-cooldown is a DELIBERATE design decision to match the backtest model that produced $0.59/day validation.

criteria.description: Final verdict: BUG / NOT BUG with quantitative justification. If BUG, concrete fix proposal + expected $/day impact (positive or negative).
criteria.evidence: VERDICT: NOT BUG. Rationale: (a) commit 9e14a4f deliberately removed trail cooldown to match backtest semantics; (b) deployed validation $0.59/day Calmar 0.029 explicitly INCLUDES re-entry events; (c) backtest re-entry events are rare (0 on ETH 297d); (d) live ZEC double re-entry is a normal high-vol-day pattern, not a defect. NO FIX needed. Adding cooldown would diverge from validated edge.
